title: SACRAMENTO – Assemblymember Pilar Schiavo and the Los Angeles County Delegation (LACD) last week voted to extend the California Film and Television Tax Credit Program contained in
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: A 2022 study by the Los Angeles Economic Development Corporation found that for every dollar allocated for the tax credit, total economic activity in California will increase by $24.40.
Since 2015, the California Film and Television Tax Credit Program has generated $22 billion in economic output.
The California Film and Television Tax Credit Program has generated $961 million in revenue from state and local taxes.
The program has paid out $8.2 billion in qualified wages to 220,000 cast and crew members hired in California.
The California Film and Television Tax Credit Program includes 1,465,000 background players.
